summary_sections.title: Key Concepts: Rock Cycle (Igneous, Sedimentary, Metamorphic)
summary_sections.content_html:
The Rock Cycle

Three Rock Types

  • Igneous: From cooled magma/lava. Intrusive (slow cooling, large crystals) vs. extrusive (fast cooling, small/no crystals).
  • Sedimentary: From compacted/cemented sediments. Clastic (broken pieces), chemical (precipitation), organic (biological remains).
  • Metamorphic: From existing rock changed by heat and/or pressure. Foliated (banded) vs. non-foliated.

Cycle Processes

  • Weathering → Erosion → Deposition → Compaction/Cementation → Uplift → Metamorphism → Melting → Crystallization
